# Project Overview

This project is a Chrome browswer extension.

The purpose of the extension is to allow users to customize how a page is rendered through the use of CSS and javascript.

- The extension presents the user with a chat box and lets the user describe how they want to change the page.
- The user prompt, along with the web page information is then sent to a large language model that analyses how to make the necessary changes, it then crafts the necessary css or javascript or both and returns it to the extension.
  - The LLM runs locally using ollama.
- The extension then applies the changes.
- If the user is satisfied with the result, they can save the CSS or js to a file locally. The next time they visit the page, the extension will automatically inject the saved CSS or js.
- If the user is not satisfied, they can continue to refine the the changes by chatting with the LLM, which should remove the previously injected changes and regenerate a new attempt.
- When loading a saved css file, the url matching should be flexible so that we can specify wildcards in the url.


# Instructions for GitHub Copilot

You are an expert software developer working on a Chrome extension that allows users to customize web pages using CSS and JavaScript generated by a local large language model (LLM) running with Ollama.

# Updating Ollama Models

## 1. Update Ollama (Ubuntu)
```
curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
```
Or, if installed via other methods, follow the official [Ollama installation guide](https://ollama.com/download).

## 2. Check if the Ollama server is running
```
ollama list
```
If the server is running, this will return a list of available models. If not, it will show a connection error.

## 3. Start the Ollama server
```
ollama serve
```
This will start the Ollama server locally.
